sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.population: Adults aged 18 years and over with cataracts, including people considering referral or surgery and people receiving postoperative review.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, optometric, ophthalmology, surgical, and postoperative cataract care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for selected cataract identity, vision and quality-of-life impact, laterality, preference-sensitive surgery discussion, postoperative information, visual-function review, and clinician-arranged follow-up fields. It does not support autonomous diagnosis, surgery or referral, medicine, dose, fixed review timing, or UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.2 — cataract information, visual function, quality of life, and referral discussion
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports cataract identity, how cataract affects vision and quality of life, whether one or both eyes are affected, tailored information, and a preference-sensitive clinician discussion about cataract surgery without making a referral decision automatically.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.5–1.1.6 and 1.9.1–1.9.3 — postoperative information and assessment
exact_sections.locator: recommendations 1.1.5–1.1.6 and 1.9.1–1.9.3; official page lines 101–136 and 473–489
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting expected visual changes, possible complication symptoms, activity or driving restrictions, contact arrangements, next appointment, vision change, spectacles, second-eye discussion, visual function, quality of life, and postoperative review processes without mapping medicines, doses, or autonomous urgency.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment — Recommendations
sources.version: NICE NG125; last updated 2020-08-19; last reviewed 2023-05-31
sources.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
sources.clinical_setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for postoperative wound-care and surgical-site-infection documentation; partial for traumatic wounds, suture removal, and UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — information, wound care, and infection recognition
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented postoperative wound-care information, infection-warning discussion, and who to contact if concern develops.

exact_sections.heading: Overview and recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.8
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the postoperative surgical-wound population and supports wound dressing, cleansing, wound-healing, and structured postoperative wound-care documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.9–1.4.11 and surgical-site-infection definition
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.
